{"author": null, "closed_at": null, "collaborating_teams": null, "collaborating_users": null, "created_at": null, "credits": [{"login": "martijnperdaan52", "type": "reporter"}], "credits_detailed": [{"state": "accepted", "type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/268664750?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/martijnperdaan52/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/martijnperdaan52/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/martijnperdaan52/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/martijnperdaan52/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/martijnperdaan52", "id": 268664750, "login": "martijnperdaan52", "node_id": "U_kgDOEAN_rg", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/martijnperdaan52/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/martijnperdaan52/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/martijnperdaan52/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/martijnperdaan52/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/martijnperdaan52/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/martijnperdaan52", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-55986", "cvss": {"score": 5.4, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:L/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 5.4, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:L/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}}, "cwe_ids": [], "cwes": [], "description": "### Summary

The API endpoints for managing user email addresses do not enforce the same credential-management restrictions as the web interface.

When administrators disable credential management for externally authenticated users (LDAP, OAuth2, SAML, etc.), the web UI correctly prevents users from adding or removing email addresses. However, the API endpoints remain accessible and allow those operations.

As a result, users can bypass administrator-imposed account management restrictions through the API.

### Details

Affected endpoints:

```http
POST /api/v1/user/emails
DELETE /api/v1/user/emails
```

The affected handlers are located in:

```text
routers/api/v1/user/email.go
```

Neither endpoint verifies whether credential management is disabled for the current authentication type.

The web UI correctly performs the following check:

```go
if user_model.IsFeatureDisabledWithLoginType(
    ctx.Doer,
    setting.UserFeatureManageCredentials,
) {
    ctx.NotFound(errors.New(
        "emails are not allowed to be changed",
    ))
    return
}
```

Equivalent protection is absent from the API implementation.

### Proof of Concept

Setup:

1. Configure LDAP/OAuth/SAML authentication.
2. Disable credential management for externally authenticated users.
3. Log in as an affected user.

The web UI correctly blocks email modification.

The API remains accessible:

```http
POST /api/v1/user/emails
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "emails": [
    "attacker-controlled@example.com"
  ]
}
```

Result:

```http
HTTP/1.1 201 Created
```

The email address is added despite administrator policy prohibiting credential changes.

The DELETE endpoint exhibits the same behavior.

### Impact

Users can bypass administrator-imposed restrictions on credential management.

In environments that rely on external identity providers, this may allow users to add or remove email addresses that administrators intended to manage centrally.

The issue represents an authorization inconsistency between the web interface and API.

title: Email Management API Bypasses ManageCredentials Feature Restrictions
